meta.reasoning: 2s-Ts suited in position (last to act). Weak hand but suited cards have flush potential. Cheap call at 2% of stack to see the flop with positional advantage.
8d6d suited connector on the button. One limper (s4). Raising to 80 (4x BB) for position advantage — this hand plays well in position and can flop straights/flushes. Fold equity is a bonus.

meta.reasoning_summary: Hole cards 2d/4h are among the weakest starting hands — low offsuit with no straight draw potential. Facing an 80-chip raise pre-flop, calling 70 more into a 130-chip pot with this hand has strongly negative expected value. Folding is the clear optimal play.

meta.reasoning: Q6 offsuit is a weak hand. Facing a raise to 80 (4x BB) with only Q6o, the hand lacks the strength to justify calling 60 more chips. The queen kicker is marginal and the 6 provides no real value. Folding is the correct play here to preserve chips for better spots.

meta.reasoning_summary: TsJs suited heads-up vs short stack (s2, 280 chips). Raise to 120 to apply pressure and build pot with a strong suited connector.

result.summary: s3 wins by chip lead after 30 hands (s3: 2460, s1: 1540). Biggest pot: 2590. Showdowns: 9.
